sn48.38:0.3: Sutta An Analysis (of the Feeling Faculties) (3)
sn48.38:1.3: The pleasure-faculty, the pain-faculty, the happiness-faculty, the distress-faculty, the equanimity-faculty.
sn48.38:2.2: Any physical pleasure, physical comfort born of body-contact to be experienced as pleasure & comfort.
sn48.38:3.2: Any physical pain, physical discomfort born of body-contact to be experienced as pain & discomfort.
sn48.38:4.2: Any mental pleasure, mental comfort born of intellect-contact to be experienced as pleasure & comfort.
sn48.38:5.2: Any mental pain, mental discomfort born of intellect-contact to be experienced as pain & discomfort.
sn48.38:6.2: Anything, physical or mental, to be experienced as neither comfort nor discomfort.
sn48.38:7.1: “With regard to this, the pleasure-faculty & happiness-faculty are to be seen as a feeling of pleasure.
sn48.38:7.2: The pain-faculty & distress-faculty are to be seen as a feeling of pain.
sn48.38:7.3: The equanimity-faculty is to be seen as a feeling of neither pleasure nor pain.
sn48.38:7.4: Thus, by this exposition, the five are three; and the three, five.”
